hooks.command: bash -c 'CMD=$(cat | jq -r ".tool_input.command // \"\""); echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "rm\s+(-[rRf]+\s+|.*\s+-[rRf]+)" && echo "{\"decision\":\"deny\",\"reason\":\"Use trash or backup instead of rm -rf\"}" >&2 && exit 2; echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(nix\s+profile\s+(install|add|remove)|cargo\s+install|pip3?\s+install|npm\s+install\s+-g)" && echo "{\"decision\":\"deny\",\"reason\":\"Use declarative config instead of imperative install\"}" >&2 && exit 2; echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+push\s+.*--force.*(main|master)" && echo "{\"decision\":\"deny\",\"reason\":\"Force push to main/master blocked\"}" >&2 && exit 2; exit 0'

hooks.prompt: Before stopping, verify: 1) Goal achieved or clearly blocked? 2) Todos complete or explained? 3) User will understand state? Return 'approve' if done. Return 'block' only if clearly incomplete.
Context compacting. Output concise summary: 1) Current goal 2) Progress (done/in-progress/remaining) 3) Key decisions 4) Open questions. Format as: ## Preserved State\n[summary]
